$ONDS gives me exposure to the autonomy layer of drones through drone-in-a-box systems, autonomous networks, counter-drone technology and command-and-control software.

Ondas could become a broader autonomous defense platform across aerial drones, counter-UAS, ground robotics, ISR, border security and other mission-critical use cases.

$ASTS plans to launch BlueBird 8, 9 and 10 aboard $SPCX Falcon 9 on June 17. 

The next-gen satellites are expected to deliver nearly 2x faster peak speeds and support voice, data and video directly to standard smartphones.

$NBIS launched a Physical AI Living Lab with $NVDA to help European robotics startups move AI-powered robots to real-world deployment.

Startups will get access to Nvidia’s Physical AI stack and Blackwell-powered infrastructure with the first cohort starting in September 2026

$KTOS gives me exposure to higher-end drone layer including tactical jet drones, propulsion & broader defense programs like Valkyrie.

The under-appreciated piece may be its engine business with management saying Turbine Technologies is “ripping” as Kratos gets pulled into more missile, drone & space programs.

$RKLB CEO Peter Beck said ~30% of everything that went to space last year had a Rocket Lab logo on it somewhere.

Rocket Lab is already far more embedded in the space economy than most people realize.

Since the War began on Feb 27th, the S&P 500 (black) is up 7.34%. But the S&P 500 without AI stocks (blue) is effectively unchanged. So, the entire S&P 500 Index rally since February has been driven by AI stocks.

But look at what happened on Friday, June 5th!

The S&P 500 was down 2.6%, its biggest daily loss since last October.

The S&P 500 without AI was up 0.02% (call it unchanged). 

So, the entire sell-off on Friday was AI stocks. The "normal world" did nothing on Friday.

@RedDogT3  WHY THIS BOUNCE COULD FAIL AGAIN
+ $AAPL & $TSLA UPDATE

The current market bounce may be running into a critical test. 

After Monday's relief rally failed near resistance and sellers regained control into the close, futures are once again pointing higher, creating a setup that looks very similar to the previous session. 

The key question is whether $SPY and $QQQ can finally push through recent highs and squeeze shorts, or whether this is another opportunity for sellers to step in and drive prices lower.

*

I discuss the importance of the $745 area on $SPY and recent highs in $QQQ as near-term decision points. 

While some traders are already discussing the possibility of a larger head-and-shoulders topping pattern developing, I want to caution against getting too far ahead of the price action. 

For now, the market appears to be consolidating after a strong run, and the recent weakness may simply represent a healthy reset that allows investors to catch their breath before the next move.

*

I also go through $AAPL in detail.

We saw a classic "sell-the-news" reaction, as $AAPL made a new high before reversing sharply and producing a bearish outside-day candle. 

Active traders who followed their sell rules had opportunities to reduce exposure as the breakout failed. 

Going forward, the key question is whether Apple sees downside follow-through below recent support levels or quickly repairs the damage and develops into a Red Dog Reversal setup. 

A fast recovery could trap late sellers and create a cleaner long opportunity, while sustained weakness could open the door to a deeper retracement.

*

$TSLA, meanwhile, showed relative strength compared with many other large-cap growth stocks. 

After reclaiming the $402-$405 resistance zone, #TSLA pushed toward the $416 area and repaired technical damage faster than many peers. 

#Tesla now sits in an important battleground zone as traders assess whether the recovery can continue. 

Some investors had speculated that Tesla could face selling pressure as a source of funds ahead of the upcoming $SPCX SpaceX IPO, but recent price action suggests that this may not be playing out, at least not yet.

*

So, not much has changed structurally. The market remains above major moving averages, leadership has been shaken but not broken, and traders should stay focused on key levels rather than forcing a directional view. 

The next few sessions will reveal whether this bounce becomes a stronger recovery or another failed rally within an ongoing consolidation.

Remember, we also have CPI and PPI this week. They will determine a lot.

*

If you found this helpful, please ❤️like and 🔁retweet

SpaceX’s IPO is reportedly well oversubscribed, with institutional orders expected to close Wednesday at 4pm ET, per Bloomberg. At $135/share, SpaceX would raise about $75B at a ~$1.8T valuation, potentially making it the largest IPO ever. Expected to price June 11 and trade June 12 under $SPCX.

One company. Two ways to trade.

Stay Tuned..

For more Information, visit graniteshares website

SpaceX reported a $4.9B net loss in 2025.

The losses are driven by heavy investment across rockets, satellites, AI infrastructure, and GPU-intensive compute.

SpaceX management describes this as a deliberate, front-loaded investment to build long-term leadership across space, connectivity, and AI.

To us, this is not new. We've seen this same playbook among public mega-cap tech companies.

For Alphabet, Amazon, Apple, Broadcom, Meta, Microsoft, Nvidia, and Oracle, total capex reached about 427 billion dollars in 2025 and is projected to rise roughly 30% to about 562 billion dollars in 2026. *Data from RBC Wealth Management as of 2/3/2026.
